channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: CHLI Global Leaders Program Fall 2026: Paid Public Policy Internship in Washington, D.C. for Students and Recent Graduates
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Applications are now open for the Fall 2026 Global Leaders Program, a prestigious semester-long paid internship designed for college students and recent graduates who are interested in gaining practical experience in public policy and leadership. The program is organized by the Congressional Hispanic Leadership Institute (CHLI) and provides participants with hands-on exposure to policymaking by […]

The post CHLI Global Leaders Program Fall 2026: Paid Public Policy Internship in Washington, D.C. for Students and Recent Graduates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the Fall 2026 Global Leaders Program, a prestigious semester-long paid internship designed for college students and recent graduates who are interested in gaining practical experience in public policy and leadership.

The program is organized by the Congressional Hispanic Leadership Institute (CHLI) and provides participants with hands-on exposure to policymaking by working in both Congressional offices and private sector organizations in Washington, D.C.

The Global Leaders Program is known for offering young professionals a unique opportunity to understand how policy decisions are developed, debated, and implemented across both the public and private sectors.

About the CHLI Global Leaders Program

The Global Leaders Program is a competitive internship initiative aimed at preparing the next generation of leaders in public policy, international affairs, and governance. Through a structured placement system, interns gain experience in government offices as well as corporate institutions, allowing them to explore how policies affect different sectors of society.

Participants benefit from direct engagement with policymakers, policy professionals, and industry leaders while working in the heart of the U.S. policy environment.

This immersive experience enables interns to develop critical policy knowledge, professional networks, and leadership skills that can shape their future careers.

Program Structure and Experience

The Fall 2026 Global Leaders Program runs for an entire academic semester and combines professional work experience with exposure to policy discussions and leadership development opportunities.

Interns will work in both Congressional offices and corporate environments, gaining a balanced understanding of how public policy interacts with business, governance, and society.

This dual-placement structure helps participants develop a comprehensive perspective on policymaking, advocacy, and policy implementation.

The program also allows interns to strengthen their professional communication, research, and analytical skills through real-world tasks and responsibilities.

Program Location and Dates

The internship takes place in Washington, D.C., one of the most influential political and policy centers in the world.

Program dates:
August 24, 2026 – December 9, 2026

During this period, interns will live and work in Washington, D.C., gaining firsthand exposure to the U.S. legislative and policy ecosystem.

Who Should Apply

The Global Leaders Program is open to motivated students and graduates who are passionate about public policy, leadership, and civic engagement.

Ideal applicants include individuals who want to build careers in public service, government, policy analysis, international relations, or advocacy.

Candidates should demonstrate strong academic performance, leadership potential, and an interest in contributing to policy discussions and solutions.

Benefits of the Program

Participants in the CHLI Global Leaders Program benefit from several valuable opportunities that can significantly enhance their professional development.

These benefits include:

Paid internship experience in Washington, D.C.
Professional exposure to Congressional and corporate offices
Hands-on experience in public policy development
Networking opportunities with policymakers and professionals
Leadership and career development opportunities

The program also helps participants build a strong professional foundation that can support future careers in government, policy research, public affairs, or international relations.

Application Deadline

The deadline to apply for the Fall 2026 Global Leaders Program is April 10, 2026.

Interested candidates are encouraged to prepare their application materials early to increase their chances of selection.

How to Apply

Prospective applicants can learn more about the program requirements and submit their application through the official link below.

For more OFY Leadership opportunities, click here.

The post CHLI Global Leaders Program Fall 2026: Paid Public Policy Internship in Washington, D.C. for Students and Recent Graduates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Nestlé Needs YOUth Mentorship 2026: Fully Funded Global Mentorship Opportunity for Young Entrepreneurs and Innovators
item.dc:creator: Susan Kamene
item.description:
Young people across the world who are passionate about entrepreneurship, innovation, and career development now have an exceptional opportunity to learn directly from global industry experts. The Nestlé Needs YOUth Mentorship 2026 program is part of a broader international initiative designed to empower young professionals, students, and aspiring entrepreneurs with the knowledge, mentorship, and skills […]

The post Nestlé Needs YOUth Mentorship 2026: Fully Funded Global Mentorship Opportunity for Young Entrepreneurs and Innovators appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Young people across the world who are passionate about entrepreneurship, innovation, and career development now have an exceptional opportunity to learn directly from global industry experts. The Nestlé Needs YOUth Mentorship 2026 program is part of a broader international initiative designed to empower young professionals, students, and aspiring entrepreneurs with the knowledge, mentorship, and skills they need to succeed in today’s competitive economy.

Organized by Nestlé through its Nestlé Youth Entrepreneurship Platform, this mentorship opportunity connects youth with experienced mentors and business leaders who can guide them in building sustainable careers, launching businesses, and developing innovative solutions for global challenges.

About the Nestlé Needs YOUth Initiative

The Nestlé Needs YOUth Mentorship program is part of the global Nestlé Needs YOUth initiative, a long-running program launched by Nestlé to improve youth employability and entrepreneurial opportunities worldwide. The initiative focuses on providing skills development, training programs, internships, mentorship opportunities, and entrepreneurship support for young people under the age of 30.

Through this initiative, Nestlé collaborates with industry partners, universities, and entrepreneurship organizations to help young individuals develop future-ready skills and gain access to meaningful economic opportunities. Since its launch, the program has supported millions of young people through training programs, digital events, internships, and mentoring initiatives.

The Nestlé Youth Entrepreneurship Platform acts as the digital hub where young innovators can access learning resources, join mentorship programs, participate in challenges, and build entrepreneurial skills required for modern careers.

What is the Nestlé Needs YOUth Mentorship 2026 Program?

The Nestlé Needs YOUth Mentorship 2026 program is designed to connect young individuals with experienced professionals from Nestlé and its partner organizations. Through structured mentorship sessions, participants gain insights into entrepreneurship, leadership, innovation, sustainability, and career growth.

The program typically includes:

  • Virtual mentorship sessions with industry experts

  • Career development guidance

  • Entrepreneurial training and skill-building workshops

  • Networking opportunities with global professionals

  • Access to learning resources and digital courses

  • Exposure to innovation and business strategy in the food and sustainability sectors

Participants are mentored by professionals who share real-world insights about building successful careers and startups.

The mentorship format may include one-on-one sessions, group workshops, and interactive discussions with senior leaders and experts from the corporate and entrepreneurial ecosystem.

Who Can Apply?

The Nestlé Needs YOUth Mentorship Program is designed primarily for young people interested in entrepreneurship, innovation, and professional development.

Eligible participants typically include:

  • University students

  • Recent graduates

  • Early-career professionals

  • Aspiring entrepreneurs

  • Youth innovators and startup founders

  • Individuals interested in sustainable business solutions

Many of the Nestlé Needs YOUth programs define youth participants as individuals between 18 and 30 years old.

Eligible Countries and Regions

The Nestlé Needs YOUth Mentorship opportunity is generally open to global participants through the Nestlé Youth Entrepreneurship Platform.

Eligible applicants may come from:

  • Africa

  • Europe

  • Asia

  • Oceania

  • North America

  • South America

Some sessions may focus on specific regions, but the digital nature of the platform allows participation from young people around the world.

Qualifications and Requirements

Applicants interested in the Nestlé Needs YOUth Mentorship 2026 program should typically meet the following criteria:

Age Requirement

Applicants are usually expected to be between 18 and 30 years old, as the program focuses on youth empowerment and employability.

Educational Background

There is no strict requirement for a specific academic discipline, but applicants often come from fields such as:

  • Business and entrepreneurship

  • Marketing and communications

  • Technology and digital innovation

  • Food science and agriculture

  • Sustainability and environmental studies

  • Engineering and data science

Students and graduates from any academic field with strong entrepreneurial interest are encouraged to apply.

Interest in Entrepreneurship and Innovation

Applicants should demonstrate interest in:

  • Startup development

  • Business innovation

  • Social entrepreneurship

  • Sustainable food systems

  • Digital transformation

  • Personal career development

Participation on the Nestlé Youth Entrepreneurship Platform

Applicants may be required to create an account on the Nestlé Youth Entrepreneurship Platform, where they can access courses, resources, and mentorship opportunities.

Benefits of the Nestlé Needs YOUth Mentorship Program

Participants selected for the mentorship program can gain numerous professional and educational benefits.

1. Direct Mentorship From Industry Leaders – Participants receive guidance from experienced professionals working at Nestlé or within its global partner network.

2. Career Development and Professional Skills – The program provides practical insights into career planning, leadership development, and workplace skills.

3. Entrepreneurial Skills Training – Participants learn how to transform innovative ideas into viable businesses.

4. Networking Opportunities – The program connects youth with entrepreneurs, industry leaders, and fellow innovators from around the world.

5. Exposure to Global Innovation Trends – Participants gain insight into emerging industries such as:

  • Sustainable agriculture

  • Food technology

  • Digital transformation

  • Environmental sustainability

6. Access to Learning Resources – Participants can access online training modules, workshops, and business development tools available on the Nestlé Youth Entrepreneurship Platform.

Program Focus Areas

The Nestlé Needs YOUth initiative focuses on three main pillars:

1. Entrepreneurship – Supporting young innovators who want to build startups or launch business ideas.

2. Employability – Providing training, mentorship, internships, and career opportunities that help young people secure jobs.

3. Agripreneurship – Encouraging youth involvement in agriculture and sustainable food production.

These areas align with Nestlé’s broader mission of strengthening the future workforce and supporting economic development among young people globally.

How to Apply

To apply for the Nestlé Needs YOUth Mentorship 2026 program, follow these steps:

1. Visit the official event page

2. Create an account on the Nestlé Youth Entrepreneurship Platform.

3. Complete your profile with your personal and professional details.

4. Register for the mentorship opportunity through the event page.

5. Wait for confirmation and further instructions regarding mentorship sessions.

Apply here

Why You Should Apply

The Nestlé Needs YOUth Mentorship program offers an invaluable opportunity for young individuals to gain mentorship from global industry leaders, develop entrepreneurial skills, and build professional networks.

With the global economy rapidly evolving, young people need access to mentorship, training, and career opportunities that help them develop future-ready skills. Programs like this provide the platform and support necessary to transform ideas into impactful businesses and careers.

Deadline

10th April 2026

If you are a young entrepreneur, student, or professional seeking guidance and exposure to international industry leaders, the Nestlé Needs YOUth Mentorship 2026 program could be a powerful stepping stone toward achieving your goals.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post Nestlé Needs YOUth Mentorship 2026: Fully Funded Global Mentorship Opportunity for Young Entrepreneurs and Innovators appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Study in New Zealand: Lincoln University International Scholarships 2026
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Applications are now open for the Lincoln University International Scholarships 2026, offering financial support to international students who wish to study in New Zealand. The scholarships are offered by Lincoln University to support talented international students pursuing undergraduate, master’s, or doctoral studies. The awards aim to help students access high-quality education while reducing tuition costs. […]

The post Study in New Zealand: Lincoln University International Scholarships 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the Lincoln University International Scholarships 2026, offering financial support to international students who wish to study in New Zealand.

The scholarships are offered by Lincoln University to support talented international students pursuing undergraduate, master’s, or doctoral studies. The awards aim to help students access high-quality education while reducing tuition costs.

Lincoln University is well known for its expertise in agriculture, environmental management, food science, and sustainability, making it one of the leading specialist land-based universities in New Zealand.

Scholarship Benefits

The Lincoln University scholarships provide different levels of financial support depending on the program and academic performance.

Benefits may include:

  • Tuition fee scholarships ranging from NZD $3,000 to NZD $10,000

  • Merit-based financial support for international students

  • Access to global research networks and academic mentoring

  • Opportunity to study in a top land-based university in New Zealand

Some fully funded opportunities are also available through the Manaaki New Zealand Scholarships, which can cover full tuition, living allowance, and additional costs.

What the Fully Funded Scholarship Covers

Under the Manaaki scholarship option, selected students may receive:

  • Full tuition fees

  • Weekly living allowance of about NZ$615

  • NZ$3,000 establishment allowance

  • Academic and research support networks

These scholarships are funded by the New Zealand government to support future global leaders and strengthen development partnerships.

Available Scholarship Types

Some of the major scholarships available at Lincoln University include:

  • International Undergraduate Scholarship

  • International Taught Master Merit Scholarship

  • Vice-Chancellor’s Scholarship

  • English for Further Study Award

Most scholarships are awarded based on academic performance, leadership potential, and personal achievements.

Eligibility Criteria

To qualify, applicants must:

  • Be international students

  • Meet academic admission requirements

  • Apply for an eligible undergraduate, master’s, or PhD program

  • Demonstrate strong academic results or leadership potential

Some scholarships may also consider country of residence, financial need, or field of study.

Study Programs

Lincoln University is particularly known for programs in:

  • Agriculture and Agribusiness

  • Environmental Science

  • Climate and Sustainability

  • Tourism and Recreation Management

  • Food Science

  • Business and Finance

Application Timeline

For many Lincoln University scholarships:

  • Semester 1 deadline: January

  • Semester 2 deadline: June

  • Summer school deadline: October

Students applying for government-funded scholarships typically apply between February and March.

Applicants are encouraged to prepare their academic transcripts, English proficiency results, and supporting documents before submitting their applications.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should apply through the official Lincoln University scholarship page.

For more Scholarship opportunities, visit OFY here.

The post Study in New Zealand: Lincoln University International Scholarships 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: RISE/E Ghana Employability Training Programme 2026: Futuremakers Accenture Skills to Succeed Initiative for Youth with Disabilities in Accra
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The Ready for Inclusive Sustainable Employment and Entrepreneurship (RISE/E) Initiative is a transformative global program aimed at promoting inclusive economic opportunities for young people with disabilities. As part of this initiative, Sightsavers, in partnership with the Ghana Blind Union (GBU) and funded by the Standard Chartered Foundation, has launched a targeted opportunity for youth with […]

The post RISE/E Ghana Employability Training Programme 2026: Futuremakers Accenture Skills to Succeed Initiative for Youth with Disabilities in Accra appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Ready for Inclusive Sustainable Employment and Entrepreneurship (RISE/E) Initiative is a transformative global program aimed at promoting inclusive economic opportunities for young people with disabilities. As part of this initiative, Sightsavers, in partnership with the Ghana Blind Union (GBU) and funded by the Standard Chartered Foundation, has launched a targeted opportunity for youth with disabilities in Ghana.

The program, known as the Futuremakers RISE/E Accenture Skills to Succeed (S2S) Programme, focuses specifically on equipping young job seekers with disabilities with essential employability skills, digital capabilities, and career readiness training to improve their chances of securing meaningful employment.

This initiative forms part of the broader RISE/E program that works across several countries including Ghana, Kenya, Pakistan, Zambia, Tanzania, and Uganda, addressing employment barriers faced by people with disabilities and supporting inclusive labor market participation.

Through this program, young people with disabilities will gain practical knowledge, job search skills, and professional development opportunities that prepare them for competitive job markets and inclusive workplaces.

Application Deadline: March 31, 2026

About the RISE/E Initiative

The Ready for Inclusive Sustainable Employment and Entrepreneurship (RISE/E) initiative was developed to address the systemic barriers that prevent young people with disabilities from accessing employment and entrepreneurship opportunities.

Globally, there are between 180 million and 220 million young people with disabilities, and nearly 80 percent live in developing countries. Many of these young individuals face significant obstacles when trying to find jobs that offer fair wages, career growth, and workplace rights.

Young women with disabilities often encounter even greater challenges due to both gender and disability-related discrimination.

The RISE/E initiative aims to break these barriers by:

  • Building employability skills

  • Supporting entrepreneurship development

  • Strengthening employer inclusion

  • Creating partnerships across labor market systems

  • Promoting disability-inclusive employment policies

The Futuremakers RISE/E Accenture Skills to Succeed (S2S) Programme – Ghana

The Futuremakers RISE/E Accenture Skills to Succeed Programme is a specialized employability training initiative designed specifically for young job seekers with disabilities in Ghana.

This program is implemented by Sightsavers in partnership with the Ghana Blind Union (GBU) and supported by the Standard Chartered Foundation under the Futuremakers initiative.

The program focuses on equipping participants with the knowledge and practical skills required to succeed in the modern workplace.

Key areas of training include:

  • Job readiness and career preparation

  • Professional communication skills

  • Workplace confidence and soft skills

  • Digital and technology-based job search tools

  • Resume and job application preparation

  • Interview preparation techniques

Through these training sessions, participants will become better prepared to navigate job markets, communicate with employers, and secure employment opportunities.

Who Should Apply

The program is specifically designed for young people with disabilities living in Accra, Ghana who are seeking employment and want to improve their employability skills.

Applicants must meet the following eligibility criteria:

  1. Be between 18 and 35 years old

  2. Be a person living with a disability

  3. Be currently residing in Accra, Ghana

  4. Demonstrate a strong interest in improving employability and job readiness

  5. Have access to a smartphone, laptop, or computer with internet connectivity

  6. Be able to dedicate at least three hours per week to participate in training activities

Applicants should be motivated to develop new skills and take active steps toward securing employment.

Why the RISE/E Ghana Programme Matters

The RISE/E Ghana employability training programme addresses a critical challenge: the lack of inclusive employment opportunities for young people with disabilities.

By providing job readiness training, career guidance, and employer engagement support, the initiative helps young people build the confidence and skills necessary to participate in the workforce.

The program contributes to:

  • Reducing unemployment among youth with disabilities

  • Promoting inclusive workplaces

  • Strengthening economic independence

  • Supporting disability-inclusive development

Ultimately, the initiative helps ensure that young people with disabilities can access dignified work and contribute meaningfully to their communities and economies.

Programme Structure and Learning Format

The Futuremakers RISE/E Accenture Skills to Succeed Programme is designed to be accessible and flexible, allowing participants to develop their skills through structured learning sessions.

Participants are expected to commit approximately three hours per week throughout the program duration.

The training focuses on practical employability development, including:

  • Career planning

  • Job search strategies

  • Professional communication

  • Workplace etiquette

  • Digital literacy

  • Interview readiness

This structure ensures that participants can balance training with other personal responsibilities while gaining valuable career development skills.

Participant Recruitment Process

To participate in the program, interested candidates must complete a short questionnaire that takes approximately 5–10 minutes.

The questionnaire helps the project team understand:

  • Applicants’ educational background

  • Current employment status

  • Skills development needs

  • Accessibility requirements

The information collected allows the program team to identify suitable candidates and provide targeted training support.

Monitoring and Evaluation of the Programme

If an applicant is selected for the program, the project team will monitor participants’ progress throughout the training period.

The program also includes a follow-up evaluation approximately six months after the training to assess whether the skills gained helped participants:

  • Improve their employability

  • Secure employment

  • Develop professional skills

Participants may be contacted for follow-up surveys if they provide consent.

Data Privacy and Participant Protection

Sightsavers is committed to protecting the privacy and confidentiality of all applicants.

Important privacy considerations include:

  • All personal data will be stored securely

  • Information provided will only be used by the project team

  • No personal details will be shared publicly

  • Participants can request that their data be removed from project records

Some questions in the questionnaire may involve personal information. However, all responses will remain confidential and will not be disclosed outside the project team.

Optional Employer Referral Opportunities

Occasionally, employers seeking to recruit people with disabilities may request assistance from Sightsavers in identifying suitable job candidates.

Participants may choose whether or not to allow the project team to share limited information with employers, including:

  • Name

  • Contact details

  • Gender

  • Education level

  • Reasonable workplace accommodation requirements

Providing this permission is optional and will not affect the application process.

Safeguarding and Ethical Standards

Participants in the RISE/E program have the right to be treated fairly, respectfully, and without discrimination or harassment.

If any participant experiences inappropriate behavior during the project, they may report concerns to the designated safeguarding contacts.

Safeguarding contacts include:

  • Nicholas Adzaho
    Email: nadzaho@sightsavers.org

  • Ruth Atengdem (Country Safeguarding Lead)
    Email: ratengdem@sightsavers.org

Participants may also report concerns through the Sightsavers Accountability Platform.

Click HERE to learn more about the RISE/E Ghana Employability Training Programme 2026.

To discover more global opportunities such as Scholarships, Grants, Fellowships, Internships, Jobs, and so on, visit OFY.

The post RISE/E Ghana Employability Training Programme 2026: Futuremakers Accenture Skills to Succeed Initiative for Youth with Disabilities in Accra appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Solar Enterprise and Skills Program (Ghana) 2026: Hands-On Solar PV Training, Entrepreneurship Skills, Mentorship, and Internship Opportunities for Youth
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The Solar Enterprise and Skills Program (SESP) 2026 is an innovative youth-focused training initiative designed to equip young people in Ghana with technical solar energy skills and entrepreneurial knowledge needed to thrive in the rapidly growing renewable energy sector. As the world transitions toward clean and sustainable energy systems, the demand for skilled professionals in […]

The post Solar Enterprise and Skills Program (Ghana) 2026: Hands-On Solar PV Training, Entrepreneurship Skills, Mentorship, and Internship Opportunities for Youth appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Solar Enterprise and Skills Program (SESP) 2026 is an innovative youth-focused training initiative designed to equip young people in Ghana with technical solar energy skills and entrepreneurial knowledge needed to thrive in the rapidly growing renewable energy sector.

As the world transitions toward clean and sustainable energy systems, the demand for skilled professionals in the solar energy industry continues to rise. The Solar Enterprise and Skills Program addresses this demand by offering practical training in solar photovoltaic (PV) installation, business development, and leadership skills, helping young people build careers or start businesses in the renewable energy industry.

Through this program, participants will gain hands-on solar training, mentorship from industry professionals, financial literacy knowledge, and potential internship opportunities with solar companies and renewable energy organizations.

Application Deadline: March 18, 2026

Overview of the Solar Enterprise and Skills Program

The Solar Enterprise and Skills Program is structured as a three-week intensive training program that combines technical solar energy training with entrepreneurial and leadership development.

The program is designed to support young people interested in renewable energy careers, clean technology innovation, and sustainable business development.

By combining technical solar training with enterprise development education, the program ensures that participants not only gain technical expertise but also understand how to create viable businesses and career opportunities in the clean energy sector.

This initiative aligns with global sustainability goals and contributes to building a skilled workforce capable of supporting the transition to renewable energy systems in Ghana and beyond.

Objectives of the Program

The Solar Enterprise and Skills Program aims to achieve several important objectives:

  1. Equip young people with practical solar PV installation skills

  2. Develop technical knowledge in solar energy systems

  3. Provide training in renewable energy maintenance and troubleshooting

  4. Support entrepreneurship development in the solar sector

  5. Improve participants’ financial literacy and leadership abilities

  6. Foster career development opportunities in the renewable energy industry

  7. Connect participants with mentors and internship opportunities

By achieving these objectives, the program helps young people become active contributors to the clean energy transition.

Program Structure and Training Format

The Solar Enterprise and Skills Program is delivered through a three-week intensive learning experience.

Participants will engage in a mix of:

  • Practical hands-on training

  • Classroom instruction

  • Mentorship sessions

  • Business development workshops

  • Industry exposure opportunities

The training curriculum focuses on building both technical expertise and entrepreneurial capacity.

Technical Training Components

Participants will receive hands-on training in the core aspects of solar photovoltaic systems.

Key technical training areas include:

1. Solar PV Installation

Participants will learn how to install solar photovoltaic systems, including:

  • Understanding solar energy principles

  • System design fundamentals

  • Installation procedures

  • Electrical connections and configuration

2. Solar System Maintenance

Participants will learn how to maintain solar energy systems effectively by understanding:

  • Preventive maintenance practices

  • Component inspection procedures

  • System performance monitoring

3. Troubleshooting Solar Systems

The training will also cover how to diagnose and resolve issues in solar systems, including:

  • Identifying faults in solar installations

  • Repair procedures

  • Safety precautions during maintenance

4. Solar Safety Protocols

Participants will be trained on important safety practices when working with solar systems, including:

  • Electrical safety standards

  • Proper use of tools and protective equipment

  • Safe installation practices

Entrepreneurship and Business Development Training

In addition to technical training, the program offers comprehensive enterprise development training.

Participants will gain knowledge and skills in areas such as:

1. Business Development in the Solar Sector

Participants will learn how to:

  • Identify market opportunities in renewable energy

  • Develop solar-based business ideas

  • Build sustainable energy businesses

2. Financial Literacy

Training will include essential financial skills such as:

  • Budgeting

  • Managing business finances

  • Pricing renewable energy services

  • Financial planning for startups

3. Leadership Development

Participants will strengthen leadership abilities through sessions focused on:

  • Decision-making skills

  • Team management

  • Professional development

4. Customer Engagement

Participants will learn strategies to engage customers effectively, including:

  • Customer relationship management

  • Marketing renewable energy solutions

  • Building trust with clients

Mentorship and Industry Exposure

An important component of the Solar Enterprise and Skills Program is the mentorship provided by experienced professionals in the renewable energy sector.

Participants will benefit from:

  • Guidance from solar industry experts

  • Insights into career pathways in renewable energy

  • Advice on building successful clean energy businesses

  • Networking opportunities within the energy sector

Internship Opportunities

Following the training program, participants may have access to internship opportunities with solar companies and renewable energy organizations.

These internships provide real-world experience in the clean energy industry and may involve:

  • Supporting solar installation projects

  • Working with renewable energy technicians

  • Gaining field experience in solar system operations

Such opportunities help participants apply their newly acquired knowledge in practical environments.

Number of Participants

The program will select twenty-five (25) motivated young participants who demonstrate interest in renewable energy and entrepreneurship.

This limited selection ensures that participants receive high-quality training and personalized mentorship.

Who Should Apply

The program is designed for young people who:

  • Are passionate about renewable energy

  • Want to develop technical solar skills

  • Are interested in entrepreneurship

  • Desire to build careers in the clean energy sector

  • Want to contribute to sustainable development in their communities

Applicants with strong motivation to create community impact through renewable energy solutions are strongly encouraged to apply.

Why the Solar Enterprise and Skills Program Matters

The Solar Enterprise and Skills Program plays a vital role in promoting youth employment, renewable energy innovation, and sustainable development.

By training young people in solar energy and entrepreneurship, the program helps to:

  • Address youth unemployment

  • Expand access to renewable energy

  • Support climate action initiatives

  • Build a skilled workforce for the green economy

Participants leave the program with the skills and knowledge needed to start businesses, secure employment, or contribute to the clean energy industry.

Application Deadline

All applications must be submitted before:

March 18, 2026

Click HERE to submit an application.

Learn more about the Solar Enterprise and Skills Program (SESP) 2026 HERE.

Discover more global opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post Solar Enterprise and Skills Program (Ghana) 2026: Hands-On Solar PV Training, Entrepreneurship Skills, Mentorship, and Internship Opportunities for Youth appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Counter Histories Puerto Rico Fellowship 2026–2027: $10,000 Grant for Photographers and Artists Exploring Memory, Archives, and Identity
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
The Magnum Foundation has launched an open call for the Counter Histories Puerto Rico Fellowship, an international program designed to support photographers and artists who engage with photography to reinterpret history and explore contemporary social questions. The fellowship invites creative practitioners working on projects connected to Puerto Rico and its diaspora to develop and complete […]

The post Counter Histories Puerto Rico Fellowship 2026–2027: $10,000 Grant for Photographers and Artists Exploring Memory, Archives, and Identity appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Magnum Foundation has launched an open call for the Counter Histories Puerto Rico Fellowship, an international program designed to support photographers and artists who engage with photography to reinterpret history and explore contemporary social questions. The fellowship invites creative practitioners working on projects connected to Puerto Rico and its diaspora to develop and complete visual storytelling projects that challenge dominant narratives and reframe historical memory.

Through a combination of financial support, mentorship, collaborative learning, and international exposure, the program aims to strengthen artistic practices that investigate archives, identity, memory, and the contested nature of history. Selected fellows will receive a $10,000 project development grant and participate in an eight-month virtual fellowship program from June 2026 to February 2027.

Applications for this opportunity are open until April 1, 2026 at 11:59 PM EDT (New York time).

About the Counter Histories Initiative

Since 2018, the Magnum Foundation’s Counter Histories initiative has supported artists who challenge dominant historical narratives and highlight suppressed or marginalized histories. The initiative explores the ways photography and visual storytelling can reveal overlooked perspectives, question historical archives, and generate new interpretations of the past.

Historical archives often contain gaps, silences, and biases shaped by power structures, colonial histories, and systemic inequalities. Counter Histories encourages artists to engage critically with these archives and develop creative responses that expand how we understand historical memory.

The initiative emphasizes collaborative research and artistic experimentation, encouraging photographers and visual artists to examine how images shape collective understanding of the past, present, and future.

Counter Histories Puerto Rico Edition

The Counter Histories Puerto Rico Fellowship focuses specifically on projects connected to Puerto Rico and Puerto Rican identity, including stories emerging from the archipelago and its global diaspora.

At a time when public memory in Puerto Rico faces challenges due to systemic disinvestment and cultural erasure, the program seeks to support artists who contribute to the creation of a collective visual archive that reflects diverse experiences and perspectives.

This new edition of the fellowship is developed through a partnership between:

  • Magnum Foundation

  • Instituto de Observación Colectiva

  • Artist and past Counter Histories Fellow Christopher Gregory-Rivera

The program is supported by the Mellon Foundation and hosted by the Museo de Arte Contemporáneo de Puerto Rico, providing fellows with opportunities to connect their projects to a broader cultural and institutional network.

Fellowship Structure and Duration

The Counter Histories Puerto Rico Fellowship combines financial support, mentorship, collaborative learning, and public presentation opportunities.

The program runs for eight months, beginning in June 2026 and concluding in February 2027.

Up to eight fellows will be selected to participate in the program.

Financial Support

Each selected participant will receive a $10,000 project development grant.

The grant is flexible and can be used to support the development and completion of the fellow’s project in ways that best fit their creative practice. Possible uses include:

  • Production costs for photography or multimedia work

  • Travel related to the project

  • Equipment and creative materials

  • Research and archival exploration

  • Editing and post-production work

In addition to the project grant, fellows will receive additional discretionary funds that can be used for:

  • Individual mentoring sessions with experts

  • Creative collaborations with other fellows

  • Specialized research support

Virtual Fellowship Program

During the fellowship period, participants will take part in a structured virtual program consisting of approximately 25–30 hours of activities spread over the eight-month period.

To ensure accessibility and flexibility, the program requires no more than four hours of participation in any given month.

The virtual fellowship may include:

  • Guest lectures by photographers, scholars, and curators

  • Collaborative working groups

  • Community-building sessions among fellows

  • Curatorial advising

  • Professional development workshops

  • Peer feedback sessions

All fellowship programming will include translation and accessibility for both English and Spanish speakers, ensuring that participants from diverse linguistic backgrounds can fully engage in the program.

In-Person Symposium

The fellowship will culminate in a public symposium, likely taking place in February 2027.

During this event, fellows will present their work and share the outcomes of their projects with a broader audience.

The Magnum Foundation will cover all travel expenses associated with attending the symposium. Additionally, the organization will ensure that fellows have reliable internet access throughout the virtual fellowship program.

Types of Projects Supported

The program supports creative production rooted in photography, particularly projects that engage critically with archives, memory, and historical narratives.

Eligible projects may include:

  • New photographic productions

  • Creative reinterpretations of historical archives

  • Artist-generated archival projects

  • Visual storytelling that activates personal or collective photographic archives

While projects may incorporate written research or archival preservation work, the program prioritizes projects where visual outputs and creative storytelling remain central.

Applicants should ensure that their project—or a significant part of it—can be completed and publicly shared by the end of the fellowship period.

Key Themes and Guiding Questions

Applicants are encouraged to explore a wide range of conceptual questions related to historical memory and visual storytelling.

These include:

  • What could an archive of the future look like?

  • How can artists engage with histories that were never photographed?

  • What creative possibilities emerge from gaps and silences in historical archives?

  • How might artists challenge photography’s role in reinforcing violent histories or limiting narratives?

  • How can emerging technologies expand photographic storytelling?

  • How can artists present alternative methods of memory-making?

  • What role does memory play in shaping archives and historical narratives?

  • How can artistic work addressing historical trauma or loss contribute to healing and reconciliation?

These questions are intended as inspiration rather than strict thematic limitations.

Who Should Apply

The fellowship is open to photographers and artists whose creative practice significantly engages with photography.

Applicants are expected to commit fully to the fellowship’s collaborative environment and contribute to the shared learning experience.

Importantly, the program has no formal education or professional experience requirements.

The organizers strongly encourage applications from:

  • Self-taught photographers and artists

  • Current students

  • Mid-career or established artists

  • Practitioners who have historically lacked access to formal training opportunities

The fellowship aims to create an intergenerational cohort representing diverse artistic backgrounds and experiences.

The organizers also encourage applications from communities that face systemic oppression or underrepresentation within the photography field.

Selection Process

Applications will be evaluated through a multi-stage review process.

All submissions will first be reviewed by the Magnum Foundation team. Final decisions will then be made by an external selection committee composed of experts in photography, visual culture, and historical research.

Fellows may be selected through either the open call process or through nominations, although all applicants will complete the same application form and receive equal review consideration.

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit several materials through the online application form.

These include:

  • Samples of current and previous work

  • A short biography

  • A short description of the proposed project

  • A list of creative inspirations

  • A statement explaining why the applicant wants to participate in the fellowship community

  • A description of what the project needs in order to be completed

Applications must be submitted as individuals, even if the project involves collaborators.

Applicants may submit materials in either English or Spanish.

Q&A Session for Applicants

A virtual information session will be held to answer questions about the fellowship and application process.

Date: March 16, 2026
Time: 12:00 PM – 1:00 PM EDT (New York)

The session will include translation and will be accessible to both English and Spanish speakers. A recording will also be made available afterward for those who cannot attend live.

For more information click here 

Apply here 

Discover more opportunities here 

The post Counter Histories Puerto Rico Fellowship 2026–2027: $10,000 Grant for Photographers and Artists Exploring Memory, Archives, and Identity appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Oak Spring Garden Foundation Plant Conservation Biology Fellowship 2027: $10,000 Grant and Residency for Early-Career Conservation Researchers
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
The Oak Spring Garden Foundation (OSGF) has opened applications for its prestigious Plant Conservation Biology Fellowship, an internationally recognized opportunity designed to support outstanding early-career professionals working in plant and landscape conservation. This highly competitive fellowship offers a $10,000 individual grant and an immersive 2–5 week residency at the Oak Spring estate in Upperville, Virginia, […]

The post Oak Spring Garden Foundation Plant Conservation Biology Fellowship 2027: $10,000 Grant and Residency for Early-Career Conservation Researchers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Oak Spring Garden Foundation (OSGF) has opened applications for its prestigious Plant Conservation Biology Fellowship, an internationally recognized opportunity designed to support outstanding early-career professionals working in plant and landscape conservation. This highly competitive fellowship offers a $10,000 individual grant and an immersive 2–5 week residency at the Oak Spring estate in Upperville, Virginia, USA, allowing fellows to dedicate focused time to research, writing, and reflection.

The fellowship aims to support promising scholars, practitioners, and scientists whose work contributes to the conservation of plants and the ecosystems and landscapes in which they exist. By offering a tranquil environment away from everyday professional demands, the program encourages fellows to advance innovative research and deepen their engagement with pressing environmental and ecological issues.

Applications for the 2027 Fellowship cycle open on February 14, 2026, and close on May 31, 2026.

About the Oak Spring Garden Foundation

The Oak Spring Garden Foundation is dedicated to preserving and sharing the legacy of Rachel “Bunny” Lambert Mellon, an influential horticulturist, philanthropist, and art patron. The foundation’s mission is to inspire scholarship, research, and dialogue about plants, gardens, landscapes, and their importance to human well-being.

Located on a 700-acre estate in Upperville, Virginia, the foundation maintains an exceptional landscape dedicated to sustainable land management and environmental stewardship. The property serves as a dynamic environment for scholars, artists, and conservation professionals who are interested in the intersection of ecology, horticulture, culture, and research.

In addition to its living landscape, the Oak Spring Garden Foundation houses the Oak Spring Garden Library, an extraordinary collection containing more than 19,000 rare books, manuscripts, botanical artworks, and historical materials related to plants, gardens, and landscapes. This library provides fellows with valuable intellectual resources that can inform their research and creative work.

The Plant Conservation Biology Fellowship is one of the foundation’s four most prestigious awards, reflecting OSGF’s strong commitment to advancing knowledge and action in plant conservation.

Fellowship Purpose and Objectives

The primary purpose of the Plant Conservation Biology Fellowship is to support early-career experts who are making meaningful contributions to the protection and sustainability of plant biodiversity.

Plant conservation is increasingly critical as ecosystems worldwide face threats from climate change, habitat loss, urban expansion, and biodiversity decline. Through this fellowship, the Oak Spring Garden Foundation seeks to empower professionals whose research and practice address these challenges.

The fellowship is particularly well suited for individuals working on writing projects, scholarly research, conservation planning, or policy-oriented work that benefits from dedicated time for deep thinking and focused intellectual work.

By removing participants from the pressures of daily professional responsibilities, the program provides the ideal environment for fellows to:

  • Reflect on ongoing research

  • Develop academic manuscripts or reports

  • Advance conservation strategies

  • Engage in interdisciplinary dialogue with fellow residents

Residency Experience at Oak Spring

Fellows will spend two to five weeks living and working on the Oak Spring estate, surrounded by its natural landscape and scholarly resources.

During their stay, fellows will have the opportunity to:

  • Explore the 700-acre landscape and observe its ecological diversity

  • Study materials within the Oak Spring Garden Library

  • Engage with OSGF staff and experts in sustainable land management

  • Participate in optional activities with other residents and fellows

The fellowship residency is scheduled to coincide with other Interdisciplinary Residents and Fellows, creating an environment of collaboration and knowledge exchange. Participants may share communal meals and optional excursions with artists, researchers, ecologists, and scholars whose work aligns with the foundation’s mission.

A typical day for a fellow might include:

  • Independent writing or research

  • Walking through the estate’s landscape to observe plant and animal life

  • Consulting rare materials in the Oak Spring Library

  • Volunteering or learning alongside staff in the formal gardens or at the Biocultural Conservation Farm (BCCF)

Participation in these activities is entirely optional. The fellowship prioritizes flexibility, allowing participants to structure their time in ways that best support their research goals.

Fellowship Presentation Opportunity

At the conclusion of their residency, fellows are encouraged—but not required—to give a short presentation about their work.

This presentation typically lasts 20 to 30 minutes and may include time for questions from OSGF staff, other fellows, and program participants. The purpose of the presentation is to facilitate dialogue and knowledge sharing among the Oak Spring community.

Financial Support and Benefits

The Plant Conservation Biology Fellowship includes a $10,000 individual grant, which provides financial support for activities related to the fellowship.

The grant can be used for:

  • Travel to and from the Oak Spring estate

  • Research materials and supplies

  • Expenses that support the fellow’s conservation work before or after the residency

The Oak Spring Garden Foundation will arrange ground transportation from Washington Dulles International Airport to the estate in Upperville, Virginia.

Fellows will stay in shared accommodations during the residency. Each fellow will have:

  • A private bedroom and bathroom

  • Shared access to a living room and kitchen with one to three other residents or fellows

This residential arrangement fosters a collaborative environment while ensuring privacy and comfort.

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit several materials through the fellowship’s online application portal.

Required application components include:

Curriculum Vitae or Resume

  • Maximum of two pages

Statement on Current Work

  • A 200–300 word description of the applicant’s current work in plant conservation.

Mission Alignment Statement

  • A 200–300 word statement explaining how the applicant’s work aligns with the mission of the Oak Spring Garden Foundation.

Residency Plan

  • A 200–300 word description explaining how the applicant intends to use their time during the residency.

Applicants should note that Oak Spring currently does not have laboratory facilities or scientific equipment, although the estate’s landscape offers extensive opportunities for ecological observation and field-based learning.

Community Engagement Statement

  • A 200-word or less statement describing a time when the applicant participated in a community, the role they played, and what they valued most about the experience.

Applicants may also indicate whether they would like to be considered for the OSGF Interdisciplinary Residency if they are not selected for the fellowship.

Residency Dates for 2027

Fellows must begin their residency on one of the scheduled dates aligned with other OSGF programs.

Available 2027 residency start dates include:

  • March 1

  • April 25 (maximum residency length of three weeks)

  • May 24

  • July 12

  • August 29 (maximum residency length of three weeks)

  • October 4

These schedules allow fellows to join orientation activities and introductory tours with other residents.

Selection Process

Applications are evaluated through a multi-round review process involving:

  • Oak Spring Garden Foundation staff

  • Alumni of previous residency programs

  • External reviewers and expert jurors

Final selections are made by panels of external experts working in fields relevant to plant conservation, ecology, landscape research, and environmental scholarship.

Key selection criteria include:

  • Alignment of the applicant’s work with the OSGF mission

  • Demonstrated potential and originality

  • Quality of previous work and writing samples

Due to the high number of applications received each year, the foundation cannot provide individualized feedback to unsuccessful applicants.

Application Deadline

The application portal will remain open until May 31, 2026.

Applicants are encouraged to begin preparing their materials early to ensure a strong submission.

For more information click here 

APPLY HERE 

Discover more opportunities here

The post Oak Spring Garden Foundation Plant Conservation Biology Fellowship 2027: $10,000 Grant and Residency for Early-Career Conservation Researchers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Advisor – Geopolitics & Geo-economics in Asia: Apply for the Friedrich-Ebert-Stiftung (FES) India Position in New Delhi
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
The Friedrich-Ebert-Stiftung (FES) India has opened applications for the position of Advisor – Geopolitics & Geo-economics in Asia, a highly strategic professional opportunity for experts interested in global politics, regional dynamics, and international policy debates. Based in New Delhi, India, this role offers the chance to work at the forefront of geopolitical analysis and policy […]

The post Advisor – Geopolitics & Geo-economics in Asia: Apply for the Friedrich-Ebert-Stiftung (FES) India Position in New Delhi appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Friedrich-Ebert-Stiftung (FES) India has opened applications for the position of Advisor – Geopolitics & Geo-economics in Asia, a highly strategic professional opportunity for experts interested in global politics, regional dynamics, and international policy debates. Based in New Delhi, India, this role offers the chance to work at the forefront of geopolitical analysis and policy dialogue in one of the most dynamic regions in the world.

With the Asia-Pacific region undergoing profound geopolitical shifts and geo-economic transformations, the position provides a platform to contribute directly to high-level policy discussions, research initiatives, and transnational collaboration efforts that shape debates about the future global order.

The position will run for an initial duration of 24 months, with a target start date between April and May 2026.

About Friedrich-Ebert-Stiftung (FES)

The Friedrich-Ebert-Stiftung (FES) is one of Germany’s oldest political foundations and is committed to promoting the values of social democracy, democratic governance, and inclusive economic development. Through its global network of offices and partnerships, the foundation engages policymakers, researchers, civil society actors, and thought leaders to develop solutions to contemporary global challenges.

In the Asia-Pacific region, FES plays a particularly active role in examining emerging geopolitical tensions and geo-economic disruptions. The organization works closely with regional experts and institutions to develop policy responses to evolving international dynamics, including shifting power structures, industrial competition, technological governance, and strategic infrastructure development.

A key approach used by FES is the development of transnational networks and transformative alliances, connecting individuals and organizations across borders to foster collaborative solutions to shared global challenges.

Position Overview

The Advisor – Geopolitics & Geo-economics in Asia will contribute to FES’s strategic initiatives by providing deep analytical insights and facilitating policy discussions related to the evolving geopolitical and economic landscape of the Asia-Pacific.

The role is based in New Delhi, a key diplomatic and strategic hub in Asia, enabling direct engagement with policymakers, international organizations, think tanks, and academic institutions.

The selected candidate will help shape policy debates around major global issues such as strategic autonomy, technology governance, and resilient supply chains.

Key Responsibilities

The Advisor will take on a range of research, project management, and policy engagement responsibilities designed to support FES’s strategic objectives in the region.

Project Development and Management

One of the core tasks of the position is the development and management of projects related to critical geopolitical and geo-economic themes in Asia and the Pacific. These projects may focus on:

  • Regional geopolitical dynamics in Asia and the Pacific

  • Strategic autonomy and geo-economic competition

  • Industrial policy and resilient global value chains

  • Technology governance and digital transformation

  • Strategic infrastructure development

The Advisor will be responsible for conceptualizing project ideas, coordinating their implementation, and ensuring their alignment with the broader strategic priorities of FES.

Geopolitical and Geo-economic Analysis

Another key responsibility is conducting in-depth analysis of regional and global trends affecting the Asia-Pacific.

The Advisor will provide analytical insights into:

  • Shifting global power structures

  • Regional geopolitical tensions

  • Geo-economic competition between major powers

  • Emerging industrial policies and supply chain transformations

  • Digital governance and technological rivalry

These analyses will help inform policy discussions and contribute to strategic debates about the future international order.

Organizing Policy Dialogues and Conferences

The position also involves organizing policy dialogues, conferences, and expert roundtables that bring together policymakers, scholars, and practitioners.

These events will provide platforms for knowledge exchange and collaboration across countries in the Asia-Pacific region. Responsibilities include:

  • Conceptualizing event themes and agendas

  • Coordinating speakers and expert participants

  • Managing logistics and administrative arrangements

  • Facilitating discussions and documenting outcomes

Such dialogues are central to FES’s mission of promoting informed and inclusive policy debates.

Network Building and Stakeholder Engagement

The Advisor will play a key role in expanding and maintaining FES networks across the region.

This includes building relationships with:

  • Policymakers and government officials

  • Think tanks and research institutes

  • Universities and academic researchers

  • Civil society organizations

  • Regional and international experts

Strengthening these networks helps ensure that FES remains connected to diverse perspectives and emerging policy ideas across Asia and the Pacific.

Strategic Communication and Policy Writing

The selected candidate will contribute to the intellectual output of FES by producing high-quality written materials.

These may include:

  • Analytical papers

  • Policy briefs

  • Strategic reports

  • Speeches and presentations

  • Communication outputs supporting public debates

Strong analytical and drafting skills in English are therefore essential for this position.

Representation in National and International Forums

As a representative of FES Asia, the Advisor may also participate in relevant national and international forums, including conferences, policy dialogues, and expert consultations.

This role allows the candidate to engage directly with influential stakeholders shaping the future of regional and global governance.

Candidate Profile

FES is seeking a candidate with strong academic training and professional experience in fields related to geopolitics, international relations, and geo-economics.

Applicants should possess:

  • A university degree in political science, international relations, economics, or a related discipline

  • 3–5 years of professional work experience

  • Demonstrated expertise in geopolitics and/or geo-economics, ideally with a focus on the Asia-Pacific region

  • Strong analytical and writing skills in English

  • Experience working with think tanks, academic institutions, international organizations, or foundations

  • The ability to manage projects independently

  • Experience working in intercultural and international environments

Additional assets include:

  • Knowledge of Asia-Pacific regional dynamics

  • German language skills, although these are not mandatory

Candidates must also possess a valid work permit for India and be willing to travel when necessary.

What FES Offers

The position provides a unique opportunity to work in one of the most strategically significant regions in global politics today.

Successful applicants will benefit from:

  • A strategically relevant role in the rapidly evolving geopolitical landscape of Asia

  • Opportunities to engage with senior policymakers, diplomats, and leading experts

  • Direct involvement in debates about the future global order

  • A collaborative and international working environment

  • Competitive compensation aligned with Friedrich-Ebert-Stiftung standards

This role offers both intellectual engagement and professional growth for individuals committed to understanding and shaping global political developments.

Application Process

Candidates interested in applying must submit the following documents:

  • A motivation letter (maximum two pages)

  • A curriculum vitae

  • Two writing samples demonstrating analytical or policy-oriented work

Application Deadline: Tuesday, 24 March 2026 (End of Business)

Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted by the organization.

For more information click here

Discover more opportunities here 

The post Advisor – Geopolitics & Geo-economics in Asia: Apply for the Friedrich-Ebert-Stiftung (FES) India Position in New Delhi appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: AFRISE Challenge 2026 for African Youth Entrepreneurs: Access $600,000+ in Technology Credits
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Applications are now open for the AFRISE Challenge 2026, an exciting startup accelerator designed to support young African entrepreneurs building innovative solutions to address social and economic challenges across the continent. The program is organized by Concordia University in partnership with Hindsight Ventures. It aims to empower youth-led startups by providing access to mentorship, technical […]

The post AFRISE Challenge 2026 for African Youth Entrepreneurs: Access $600,000+ in Technology Credits appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the AFRISE Challenge 2026, an exciting startup accelerator designed to support young African entrepreneurs building innovative solutions to address social and economic challenges across the continent.

The program is organized by Concordia University in partnership with Hindsight Ventures. It aims to empower youth-led startups by providing access to mentorship, technical support, and global exposure within the innovation ecosystem.

The AFRISE Challenge is designed to strengthen Africa’s growing startup landscape by supporting founders who are creating scalable ventures that can drive economic transformation. Selected startups will participate in a 4-week virtual accelerator program, gaining expert guidance and access to valuable resources that can help them scale their businesses.

At the end of the program, participants will have the opportunity to showcase their ventures during an International Virtual Demo Day, where they can pitch their ideas to investors, partners, and global stakeholders.

Program Benefits

Selected participants in the AFRISE Challenge 2026 will receive a range of benefits to support their startup growth and innovation journey.

These benefits include:

  • Access to more than $600,000 in technology credits

  • Participation in a 4-week intensive virtual accelerator program

  • Mentorship from global founders, investors, and industry experts

  • Exposure to international innovation ecosystems

  • Opportunity to pitch solutions at a global Virtual Demo Day

The program provides early-stage entrepreneurs with the support needed to transform their ideas into scalable businesses capable of addressing real-world challenges.

Focus Areas

The AFRISE Challenge is seeking startups that are developing innovative solutions across key sectors that are essential to Africa’s development.

Eligible focus areas include:

  • Financial technology and digital financial services

  • Health innovation and health technologies

  • Agriculture and agritech solutions

  • Energy and climate innovation

  • Education technology

  • Environmental sustainability solutions

  • Telecommunications and creative industries

Projects should demonstrate strong innovation, scalability, and the potential to create measurable social or economic impact.

Eligibility Criteria

The AFRISE Challenge 2026 is open to young African innovators and startup founders.

Applicants must:

  • Be between the ages of 18 and 35

  • Be an entrepreneur or part of a startup team developing a scalable solution

  • Be building a solution that addresses social or economic challenges

  • Be located in one of the eligible African countries

Applicants should demonstrate strong entrepreneurial potential, innovative thinking, and the capacity to scale their ventures.

Eligible Countries

Entrepreneurs from the following countries are eligible to apply:

  • Nigeria

  • Kenya

  • Rwanda

  • Ghana

  • Senegal

  • Morocco

  • Uganda

  • Côte d’Ivoire

Application Deadline

The deadline to apply for the AFRISE Challenge 2026 is:

March 27, 2026

Interested applicants are encouraged to prepare their materials early and submit their applications before the deadline.

How to Apply

Applicants can submit their application through the official program link .

For more Fellowship/ startup accelerator opportunities from OFY, Visit Here.

The post AFRISE Challenge 2026 for African Youth Entrepreneurs: Access $600,000+ in Technology Credits appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: AREF Research Development Fellowship 2026/2027: Up to £47,000 for African Early Career Health Researchers to Train at Leading Institutions in the UK, Europe, or Africa
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The Africa Research Excellence Fund (AREF) has officially opened applications for the 2026/2027 Research Development Fellowship Programme, a prestigious opportunity designed to strengthen the capacity of early-career African researchers working on critical human health challenges. This fellowship is specifically designed to help emerging African research leaders enhance their skills, build international collaborations, and accelerate their […]

The post AREF Research Development Fellowship 2026/2027: Up to £47,000 for African Early Career Health Researchers to Train at Leading Institutions in the UK, Europe, or Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Africa Research Excellence Fund (AREF) has officially opened applications for the 2026/2027 Research Development Fellowship Programme, a prestigious opportunity designed to strengthen the capacity of early-career African researchers working on critical human health challenges.

This fellowship is specifically designed to help emerging African research leaders enhance their skills, build international collaborations, and accelerate their academic careers. Selected fellows will receive funding of up to £47,000 and gain access to a three to nine-month research placement at a leading institution in the United Kingdom, Europe, or Africa.

The AREF Research Development Fellowship aims to support talented researchers who are committed to improving health outcomes in Africa by strengthening their research capabilities and leadership potential.

Application Deadline: 08 May 2026 at 13:00 GMT

About the Africa Research Excellence Fund (AREF)

The Africa Research Excellence Fund (AREF) is a charitable organization dedicated to supporting the development of world-class research leadership across Africa, particularly in areas related to human health and biomedical research.

AREF’s mission is to help African researchers develop the skills, networks, and opportunities needed to become independent leaders in global health research.

Through targeted fellowships, training programmes, and mentoring opportunities, AREF seeks to strengthen Africa’s research ecosystem and contribute to evidence-based solutions for major health challenges affecting the continent.

Aim of the AREF Research Development Fellowship

The primary goal of the AREF Research Development Fellowship (RDF) is to support early-career African researchers who are emerging leaders in health research.

The programme aims to:

  • Develop the research skills and leadership capacity of African scientists.

  • Enable researchers to gain exposure to world-class research environments.

  • Foster international collaborations and mentorship opportunities.

  • Strengthen research capacity within African institutions.

  • Support innovative research addressing important human health challenges affecting Africa and the world.

The fellowship helps researchers gain practical experience that will allow them to advance their research careers and contribute meaningfully to global health science.

What the Fellowship Offers

The AREF Research Development Fellowship provides significant financial and professional support to selected fellows.

Financial Support

Successful applicants can receive up to £47,000, which may cover:

  • Accommodation during the placement

  • Travel expenses

  • Subsistence costs

  • Training expenses

  • Research-related costs

It is important to note that AREF does not reimburse the fellow’s employing institution for their salary during the placement.

Research Placement Opportunity

A key feature of the fellowship is the opportunity to undertake a three to nine-month placement at a leading research institution.

Possible host institutions include those located in:

  • The United Kingdom

  • Europe

  • Africa

During this placement, fellows will work closely with experienced researchers and mentors to develop advanced research skills, expand their professional networks, and gain exposure to international research environments.

Additional Support Before and After Placement

The fellowship programme is designed to provide long-term career development support.

Support includes:

  • Preparation activities before the placement

  • Professional development during the fellowship

  • Continued support and mentoring after returning to the fellow’s home institution

This ensures that fellows can transfer newly acquired knowledge and skills back to their institutions in Africa, thereby strengthening local research capacity.

Why This Fellowship Matters

The AREF Research Development Fellowship represents one of the most important capacity-building opportunities for African health researchers.

The programme helps:

  • Strengthen research leadership across Africa

  • Build sustainable research careers

  • Promote international collaboration

  • Improve health research outcomes across the continent

By empowering emerging African researchers, AREF contributes to long-term improvements in global health research and innovation.

Fellowship Timeline

The AREF Research Development Fellowship follows a structured timeline.

Key Dates

  • Call for applications: Open now

  • Application deadline: 08 May 2026 (13:00 GMT)

  • Outcome notification: December 2026

  • Fellowship start window: June 2027 – December 2027

Successful fellows must begin their fellowship placement within this timeframe.

Who Can Apply

The fellowship is targeted at early-career African researchers who are actively engaged in research related to human health.

Eligible applicants include:

  • Postdoctoral scientists

  • Clinicians engaged in research

  • Early career academics working in health-related fields

Applicants must demonstrate strong research potential and leadership capability.

Eligibility Criteria

To qualify for the AREF Research Development Fellowship, applicants must meet several key requirements.

Nationality and Employment

Applicants must:

  • Be nationals of an African country

  • Be employed by an African research or academic institution

Career Stage

Eligible candidates must be:

  • Early career researchers

  • Postdoctoral scientists or clinicians with advanced research qualifications

  • Actively involved in health research

Research Focus

The applicant’s research must address important challenges related to human health.

The fellowship supports research areas that contribute to:

  • Improving health outcomes

  • Advancing biomedical research

  • Addressing major public health challenges

  • Developing innovative healthcare solutions

Host Institution Requirements

Applicants must secure a host research institution for their fellowship placement.

Host institutions may be located in:

  • The United Kingdom

  • Europe

  • Africa

It is strongly recommended that applicants engage with their host institutions early in the application process to ensure alignment with their research objectives.

Institutional Support Requirement

Applicants are also required to discuss their fellowship plans with senior leaders within their employing institution.

Support from the home institution is essential because:

  • Fellows will be away for 3 to 9 months

  • Institutional backing ensures successful reintegration after the fellowship

  • Senior mentors may provide career guidance during the programme

Application Process

Applicants must carefully review the official guidance documents before starting their application.

Steps to Apply

  1. Review the Scheme Information document

  2. Read the Application Guidance

  3. Prepare the research proposal and required documents

  4. Submit the completed application through the online portal

Applications must be submitted via the official AREF fellowship portal.

Application Portal:
https://programmes.aref-africa.org.uk/

Guidance Documents Available

Applicants are encouraged to consult the following documents before applying:

  • Scheme Information

  • Guidance on Completing Application Forms

  • AREF Terms and Conditions

These resources provide detailed information about eligibility, application preparation, and fellowship expectations.

Contact Information

Applicants who require additional information may submit questions through the Contact Us section of the fellowship portal.

Applicants are encouraged to submit questions before 27 April 2026.

Click HERE to learn more about the Africa Research Excellence Fund (AREF) and discover more global fellowship opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post AREF Research Development Fellowship 2026/2027: Up to £47,000 for African Early Career Health Researchers to Train at Leading Institutions in the UK, Europe, or Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
